author: ananta dāsa
verses:
preme matta nityānanda, sahaje ānanda kanda,
ḍhuliyā ḍhuliyā cali’ jāya
bhāiyāra bhāvete matta, jānena sakala tattva,
hari bali’ avanī loṭāya

nitāira gorāpreme gaḍā tanukhāni
gadādhara mukha here, loliyā loliyā paḍe,
dhārā vahe siñcita dharaṇī

advaita ānanda kanda, heri’ nitāira mukha-candra,
huṅkāra pulaka śobhā pāya
hari hari bola bale, punaḥ ‘gaura’ ‘gaura’ bale,
priya pāriṣada-gaṇa dhāya

golokera prema-vanyā, jagat karila dhanyā,
atula apāra rasasindhu
mātila jagat bhari’, nitāi caitanya kari’,
anantadāsa māge eka bindu
en_translation:
Lord Nityānanda is intoxicated with divine love. He is naturally the root of all joy. He sways and moves along, absorbed in the mood of a brother to Lord Caitanya. He knows all truths. Crying "Hari!", He rolls on the ground.

Lord Nitāi's body is made of the divine love of Lord Gaurāṅga. Seeing the face of Gadādhara Paṇḍita, He becomes overwhelmed and falls down, and a stream of tears flows, sprinkling the earth.

Advaita Ācārya, the root of joy, sees the moon-like face of Lord Nitāi and roars with ecstatic joy. He chants "Hari! Hari! Bol!" and again says "Gaura! Gaura!". The beloved associates run toward Them.

The flood of divine love from Goloka has made the world blessed. It is an incomparable, boundless ocean of divine mellows. The whole world became mad with love through Nitāi and Caitanya. Ananta Dāsa begs for a single drop.
